trade.summary: Cina is the Paesi Bassi' third-largest trading partner. Paesi Bassi is a major European entry point for Chinese goods via Rotterdam. Paesi Bassi exports high-tech equipment (ASML chip machines), food products, and chemicals. Cina exports electronics, machinery, and consumer goods. The semiconductor equipment trade is of unique strategic significance.
disputes: Dutch export controls on ASML EUV/DUV machines to Cina (aligned with Stati Uniti)
Chinese opposition to Dutch semiconductor export restrictions
Dutch concerns about Chinese espionage (especially at ASML)

diplomatic.summary: Relations have been severely strained by the Paesi Bassi' decision to restrict exports of ASML semiconductor equipment to Cina, aligned with Stati Uniti export controls. Cina has repeatedly pressured the Paesi Bassi to reverse this policy. The Paesi Bassi is a vocal critic of Cina on human rights (Xinjiang, Hong Kong) and has expelled Chinese intelligence officers. Dutch public opinion has become more negative toward Cina.
recent_incidents: Paesi Bassi restricted ASML EUV exports to Cina (2023)
Paesi Bassi expanded export controls to older DUV ASML machines (2024)
Cina retaliated with restrictions on gallium and germanium exports critical for chip manufacturing
Dutch intelligence expelled Chinese spies targeting ASML
Paesi Bassi aligned with EU EV tariffs

military.summary: No military alliance. Paesi Bassi is a NATO member and Cina is designated a systemic challenge by NATO. Paesi Bassi participates in South Cina Sea freedom of navigation operations. No arms transfers (EU embargo). Dutch frigate operated alongside Stati Uniti carrier near Taiwan (2024).
incidents: Dutch frigate participated in Taiwan Strait transit (2024)
Netherlands joined US-led semiconductor export control coordination

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment. Chinese companies use Dutch holding structures for European operations. Dutch pension funds are reducing Cina exposure. Chinese investment in Dutch tech companies is under increasing scrutiny. Rotterdam remains a major hub for Chinese trade with Europe.

energy.summary: Paesi Bassi imports Chinese solar panels and batteries. Dutch Shell and other energy firms have Cina operations. Some LNG trade via Rotterdam. Cina imports Dutch food and agricultural products.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation under growing scrutiny. Dutch universities are reviewing Chinese partnerships for dual-use risks. ASML technology espionage cases have heightened concern. Joint publications declining as Paesi Bassi tightens research security.
